Winning creative variants

Why this works

Boost Boss is the only ad network that sees real-time intent in AI-native surfaces. Other networks match on cookies and keywords; Benna matches on what the user is actually trying to do. That's why letting Benna allocate beats hand-picking placements — your dollar buys a better-matched eyeball.

Brand kit + creative library

Everything Benna shows your audience. Company identity stamps every impression with brand trust; creative variants give Benna's bandit room to find what wins. Edit anytime — changes apply to the next auction round.

Company identity

Who's behind the product. Appears under "Sponsored by" on every placement.

Company details

Surfaces on hover cards + product page. Optional but helps brand trust.

Founder card

Real person + photo lifts trust by ~25% on AI-app placements. Optional but recommended.

Creative library

Variants Benna's multi-arm bandit will test. Aim for 3+ headlines — the minimum for meaningful A/B/n testing.

Max 10, 240 chars each. Benna picks one per impression.
Falls back to your product description if empty.
Falls back to "Learn more" if empty.

Trust signals

Shown next to the CTA where space allows. Lowers checkout friction.

Need to update images or video? Hero images and the demo video are still managed in the product's Basics + Marketing tabs. Benna picks from those automatically.
Changes apply on the next auction round. Auto-save disabled — hit save to push.

Setup

One-time configuration · pixel and rules
Conversion tracking
Not detected yet

Track conversions on your site to unlock Benna's CPA and ROAS optimization. Add this snippet before the closing </head> tag on your conversion page. 

<script async src="https://boostboss.ai/pixel.js"
  data-advertiser-id="YOUR_ADVERTISER_ID"></script>
<script>
  window.bbq = window.bbq || [];
  bbq.push(['track', 'signup', { value: 29.99, currency: 'USD' }]);
</script>
Test end-to-end: /pixel-test.html →
Automated rules

Trigger actions automatically when performance crosses thresholds — pause campaigns, shift budget, raise bids on winning audiences.

New Product

Preview live page ↗
Click 🎬 Upload to attach an mp4 / webm / mov. When set, takes the primary visual slot on the buyer page; the image carousel below still works.
Click 📷 Upload to pick a file, drag it onto the field, or paste a URL.
💰 Pricing lives in the Pricing & Affiliate tab
Add your pricing tiers explicitly (Starter / Pro / Team, or whatever fits). One tier is fine for a single-plan product; multiple tiers ship as a plan picker on the buyer page. You won't be able to submit for audit until at least one tier has a proof URL.
Additional hero images (optional — up to 8 URLs, one per line)
Buyer page shows your primary image above + these as clickable thumbnails below it (AppSumo-style carousel). Use imgur, Cloudinary, or your own CDN — paste each URL on its own line.
When set, the BB product page shows a "Read full details →" link out to your own site.
TL;DR (top-of-page bullets)
Short value-prop sentences shown as green-check bullets at the top of your product page. AppSumo / Castmagic style. 2–5 bullets, one per line.
At-a-glance card
3-column info box buyers scan in a second. One item per line in each field.
Feature blocks (zigzag content)
The image-left/image-right blocks that fill most of the page (AppSumo / Castmagic style). Each block = heading + 2-4 bullets + screenshot. Use up to 6 blocks for best effect.
Format: first line of each block = heading. Lines starting with - or • = bullets. Line starting with Image: or any https:// URL = block screenshot. Separate blocks with a blank line.
Trust block
Refund window drives the "Refundable up to N days" badge buyers see next to the price. Deal terms render as a bottom-of-page accordion. Buyers read this before clicking Buy.
Company card
The brand block at the bottom of your product page. AppSumo shows logo, founded date, location, team size, growth stage, funding, and an "about" paragraph. All optional but each one adds trust.
Founder card
The "made by a real person" signal. Photo + LinkedIn does a lot of work in the buyer's head.
How activation works
View full integration guide →
After payment, BB emails the buyer a one-click "Activate your account" button linking to your signup page with the token appended as ?bb_token=<code>. Your signup endpoint validates the token via BB's API and creates the account already on the paid plan — no separate redemption page needed.
Usually your signup / register page. BB appends ?bb_token=<code> when the buyer clicks the email CTA. Your signup endpoint should:
1. Read bb_token from the URL
2. On form submit, POST it to /v1/vouchers/validate with your seller secret
3. If valid, create the account already on the paid plan + call /v1/vouchers/redeem to mark consumed

Two-path support: handle both "new email → create account" and "existing email → log in + upgrade plan". The buyer's purchase email is in the validate response — feel free to pre-fill it or let the buyer use a different one.
No backend? Use the Zapier path instead — wire the fulfillment webhook to your CRM.
BB fires a signed POST here when a payment captures. Lets you pre-provision before the buyer hits the redemption page.
Days buyer has to redeem voucher.
For subscription packs: how long access lasts after redemption.
Affiliate marketing budget
Pick what % of each sale funds your affiliate program. BB takes 30% of that budget · your affiliate gets 70% · you keep the rest of the sale. Higher pool = stronger affiliate motivation.
On a $100 sale: $80 to you · $14 to affiliate · $6 to BB
Pricing plans
Multiple tiers ship to the buyer page as a plan picker. Each plan needs original_price + proof URL before going live.
Save the product first, then add pricing plans here.
New plan
Creative variants for Benna
Give Benna multiple headlines, body lines, and CTAs to test. The auction's multi-arm bandit picks the winning combination per placement. Aim for 3+ headlines — that's the minimum for meaningful A/B/n testing.
Max 10 headlines, 240 chars each. Benna picks one per impression.
Max 10 lines, 240 chars each. Falls back to your product description if empty.
Max 10 labels, 60 chars each. Falls back to "Learn more" if empty.
Images and video? Already covered. The hero images field on the Basics tab feeds image variants. Your demo video on the Marketing tab is the video creative. Benna picks from those just like the text variants here.
🔒 This product is approved and locked. To keep the marketplace honest, approved products are immutable — sellers can't edit pricing or marketing after approval. Use Duplicate as new to create a fresh version pre-filled with these values, then update + resubmit for audit. Learn more →
Tab fields marked optional can be filled later — save anytime.

AI App UA Configuration

How CPI works: You pay your bid only when a publisher's user installs your AI app and your pixel confirms the install. Your ad appears on AI apps whose users match your target intent — e.g., "writing assistant" users discover your AI editor. You won't be charged for impressions or clicks that don't convert.

Only conversions whose type matches this list will be charged. Configure conversion firing in the conversion beacon docs.

B
Benna optimization
Set a goal. The engine handles bidding.
Powered by Benna
1
01 · tCPA
Target CPA
Hit a cost per acquisition. Benna tunes bids in real time.
2
02 · tROAS
Target ROAS
Optimize for return on ad spend. Ideal for revenue campaigns.
3
03 · MaxConv
Max Conversions
Spend the full budget on the highest-probability converters.
4
04 · Manual
Manual bid
You set a fixed bid. Benna signals still applied to targeting.
$ per conversion
× return on spend
Benna will score every eligible impression against your targeting signals and auto-bid up to a ceiling that hits your target CPA of $4.50. Expect a learning phase of 48–72 hours while the engine calibrates on your conversion events, then steady optimization. Live adjustments viewable in the campaign dashboard.
$
